1.description: Illustration of the Timeline for Our Model

2.description: (Color online) The Effect of α on (a) the Optimal Coupon Scheme ρ ∗ ( X ) ; (b) the Bankruptcy Trigger X B * ( I * ( X ) , ρ * ( X ) ) ; and (c) the Optimal Investment Size I ∗ ( X ) for Large Initial Demand ( X = 9) in the BO Scenario (Solid Lines) and the NBO Scenario (Dashed Lines) Notes . In panel (c), the red line depicts the firm’s optimal investment size in the BO case if the lender would offer the same coupon rate as in the NBO case. μ = 0.03 , r = 0.1 , σ = 0.1 , δ = 40 , and η = 0.02 .

3.description: (Color online) Effect of α on the Value of (a) the Lender and (b) the Firm for the Scenarios with (Solid Lines) and without (Dashed Lines) the Bankruptcy Option for Large Initial Demand ( X = 9) Notes . The red lines depict the lender’s and firm’s value in the BO case if the lender would offer the same coupon rate as in the NBO case. μ = 0.03 , r = 0.1 , σ = 0.1 , δ = 40 , and η = 0.02 .

4.description: (Color online) Effect of α on (a) the Equilibrium Investment Threshold X F ∗ , (b) the Coupon Rate ρ ∗ ( X F ∗ ) , and (c) the Investment Size I opt for the Scenarios with (Solid Lines) and without (Dashed Lines) the Bankruptcy Option for Small Initial Demand ( X = 5) Notes . In panel (a), the gray dashed line shows X ˜ F , and the gray dashed-dotted line shows X ˜ D . The red lines are generated assuming that the lender commits to the NBO coupon scheme ρ NBO ( · ) and that the firm reacts optimally to that. μ = 0.03 , r = 0.1 , σ = 0.1 , δ = 40 , and η = 0.02 .

5.description: (Color online) Effect of α on (a) the Value of the Lender and (b) the Value of the Firm for Small Initial Demand ( X = 5) and σ = 0.1 in Scenarios with (Solid Lines) and without (Dashed Lines) the Bankruptcy Option Notes . The red lines are generated assuming that the lender commits to the NBO coupon scheme ρ NBO ( · ) and that the firm reacts optimally to that. The dotted lines indicate the value of the lender and of the firm if X F * = X ˜ F for all α . μ = 0.03 , r = 0.1 , δ = 40 , and η = 0.02 .

6.description: Effect of α on Social Welfare for σ = 0.1 in Scenarios with (Solid Lines) and without (Dashed Lines) the Bankruptcy Option for (a) Large Initial Demand ( X = 9) and (b) Small Initial Demand ( X = 5) Note . μ = 0.03 , r = 0.1 , δ = 40 , and η = 0.02 .

7.description: Effect of α on Social Welfare for σ = 0.2 in Scenarios with (Solid Line) and without (Dashed Line) the Bankruptcy Option Note . μ = 0.03 , r = 0.1 , δ = 40 , X = 5 , and η = 0.02 .

8.description: Combinations of α and σ That Mitigate Double Marginalization under (a) Large Initial Demand ( X = 9) and (b) Small Initial Demand ( X = 5) Note . μ = 0.03 , r = 0.1 , δ = 40 , and η = 0.02 .
